features.description: Create comprehensive benchmark task definitions as YAML files organized by category (software-engineering, planning, product-mind, startup-mind). Each task must have: id, category, title, description, context (referencing a real ~/dev project), prompt, input_format, output_format, grading (rubric or reference_answer), difficulty (easy/medium/hard), estimated_time_minutes. Minimum: 12 software-engineering, 10 planning, 10 product-mind, 10 startup-mind tasks. Tasks must cover: code debugging, refactoring, system design, API design, requirements analysis, decomposition, user empathy, prioritization, business models, go-to-market strategy, resource allocation, competitive analysis.
preconditions: Task YAML schema defined in tasks/schema.yaml
expectedBehavior: 40+ task YAML files created across 4 categories
Each task references a specific real project from ~/dev as context
Tasks cover multiple programming languages (Python, TypeScript, Go, Rust, etc.)
Each task has complete grading rubric or reference answer
Tasks span easy/medium/hard difficulty levels
Task YAML files validate against schema
verificationSteps: ls tasks/ - count files per category (min 10 each)
python -c 'import yaml; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in glob("tasks/**/*.yaml", recursive=True)]' - no errors
grep -r 'project_ref' tasks/ - verify project references exist
grep -rl 'reference_answer\|rubric' tasks/ - verify grading defined

features.description: Build the Python benchmark runner CLI as an installable package. Must support: `llm-bench --help`, `llm-bench run --task <id> --model <model>`, `llm-bench run --all --model <model>`, `llm-bench results <run_id>`. Must integrate with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google AI APIs via litellm. Must produce JSON results files with per-task scores, category breakdowns, and overall weighted score. Must handle API errors gracefully with retries.
preconditions: Task YAML files exist in tasks/ directory
expectedBehavior: pip install -e . succeeds
llm-bench --help shows all available commands
llm-bench run --task <id> --model <model> executes single task and outputs scored result
llm-bench run --all runs all tasks and produces results/<run_id>/results.json
Results JSON contains: model_name, run_id, timestamp, per_task_results[], category_scores{}, overall_score
API errors trigger retry (max 3) with exponential backoff
Results are saved atomically (write to .tmp then rename)
verificationSteps: pip install -e . && llm-bench --help | grep -c 'run\|results\|task' (expect 3+)
llm-bench run --task SE-001 --model gpt-4o --dry-run (verify task loading)
llm-bench run --all --model gpt-4o --dry-run (verify all tasks load)
Verify results JSON schema matches expected format

features.description: Implement the grading system supporting two modes: (1) Auto-grading: compare LLM output against reference answers using exact match, structural match, and LLM-as-judge fallback. (2) Rubric-based grading: score against multi-criteria rubrics with weighted sub-scores. Grading output must include: total_score (0-100), per_criterion_scores{}, grading_rationale, raw_llm_output, reference_comparison.
preconditions: Tasks have reference_answer or rubric fields defined
Benchmark runner core executes tasks and captures output
expectedBehavior: Auto-graded tasks with reference answers produce deterministic scores
Rubric-graded tasks score each criterion independently (0-10 per criterion)
LLM-as-judge fallback uses a strong model (e.g., gpt-4o) for open-ended evaluation
Grading results include full rationale for human review
Grading is reproducible (same input → same score)
verificationSteps: Run auto-graded task twice, verify identical scores
Run rubric-graded task, verify each criterion scored independently
Verify grading output JSON schema

features.description: Build a Next.js web dashboard for visualizing benchmark results. Pages: (1) Home: table of all runs with model names, overall scores, dates. (2) Run Detail: per-category radar chart, per-task score table, model info. (3) Task Detail: raw LLM output, reference answer, grading breakdown. (4) Comparison: side-by-side model comparison with delta scores. Import: drag-drop JSON results file. Styling: clean, minimal, professional (Tailwind CSS).
preconditions: Results JSON files exist from benchmark runs
expectedBehavior: pnpm dev starts server on port 3200
Home page loads and shows runs table
Category breakdown shows as radar chart with per-category scores
Clicking a task shows detail view with raw output and grading
Comparison view shows side-by-side model scores with delta
JSON file drag-drop imports and displays results
Dashboard is responsive (mobile + desktop)
verificationSteps: pnpm dev && curl -s http://localhost:3200 | grep -c '<' (verify HTML loads)
Manual: agent-browser visits localhost:3200, verifies table renders
Manual: upload a results JSON file, verify it displays
Manual: compare two models side-by-side

features.description: Add `llm-bench task create` command that scaffolds a new task YAML from a template, and `llm-bench task validate` that validates all task YAML files against the schema. Template must include all required fields with example values. Validator must check: required fields present, valid category, valid difficulty, valid grading type, referenced project exists in ~/dev.
expectedBehavior: llm-bench task create --category software-engineering --id SE-NEW scaffolds template
llm-bench task validate validates all tasks and reports errors
Template includes comments explaining each field
Validator checks project_ref exists as directory in ~/dev
verificationSteps: llm-bench task create --category planning --id PLAN-NEW && cat tasks/planning/PLAN-NEW.yaml
llm-bench task validate (expect all pass)
Manually break a task YAML file and validate (expect error)

features.description: Wire up the complete pipeline: task definitions → runner → grading → results → web dashboard. Create sample benchmark run with at least 2 different models (if API keys available, otherwise dry-run mode with mock responses). Ensure results flow correctly from execution through grading to web display. Add `llm-bench report export` to generate markdown summary report.
preconditions: API keys available for at least one model (or dry-run mode works)
expectedBehavior: llm-bench run --all --model <model> produces valid results
Results load correctly in web dashboard
llm-bench report export generates readable markdown summary
Full pipeline: run → results → dashboard → comparison works end-to-end
Results persist and can be reloaded in web UI
verificationSteps: llm-bench run --all --model gpt-4o-mini --dry-run (or real if key available)
Open web dashboard at localhost:3200, verify results display
llm-bench report export > report.md && cat report.md
Full end-to-end: run → load in dashboard → compare → export report
